mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate over the prior month signals easier discount conditions and supports equity multiples, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 20 trading days when policy is easing.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of months with a 1-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if the rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent level signals weakening labor demand and deteriorating growth expectations, which should pressure risk assets and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2pp versus 3 months earlier
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not have below-zero median 20-day SPX returns, or if positive returns occur in more than 60% of triggers across any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous acceleration in PAYEMS and RSAFS signals broadening household income and retail demand, which lifts earnings expectations and should generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not outperform either series alone in out-of-sample Rank IC, or if 60-day forward returns are not positive in triggered expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in INDPRO with stable labor conditions indicates improving real output without immediate overheating, which should support equities over the next 20 trading days through better earnings breadth.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises by at least 0.5% month over month while UNRATE is unchanged or lower
failure_condition: Reject if positive SPX returns are not more frequent under the joint trigger than under INDPRO alone, or if the trigger fails to produce a positive median 20-day return.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is elevated, a continued rise in CPIAUCSL implies real-rate pressure and a tighter policy reaction function, which should lower S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.0% and CPIAUCSL increases for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint inflation-policy trigger does not produce negative 5-day median returns, or if the signal becomes positive in a majority of triggered episodes over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A low unemployment rate accompanied by expanding PAYEMS indicates a tight but still improving labor market, which supports consumer income and equity demand, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE below 4.0% and PAYEMS rises over the prior month
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward returns are not above baseline when the joint labor condition is met, or if the effect disappears after controlling for PAYEMS alone.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST signals weakening housing demand and lower future consumption, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as cyclical growth expectations soften.
failure_condition: Reject if negative HOUST changes do not precede below-zero median 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign flips in more than half of rolling 3-year subperiods.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, rising INDPRO with stable or falling UNRATE signals that supply is healing without a labor-market downturn, so S&P 500 returns should be positive over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint supply-labor pattern fails to deliver positive 20-day median returns, or if its predictive power is weaker than a random baseline in walk-forward testing.
